SEARCH
Package it.unipi.tonightscall.repository.document

Interface OrganizerRepository

All Superinterfaces:
org.springframework.data.repository.CrudRepository<Organizer,String>, org.springframework.data.repository.ListCrudRepository<Organizer,String>, org.springframework.data.repository.ListPagingAndSortingRepository<Organizer,String>, org.springframework.data.mongodb.repository.MongoRepository<@NonNull Organizer,@NonNull String>, org.springframework.data.repository.PagingAndSortingRepository<Organizer,String>, org.springframework.data.repository.query.QueryByExampleExecutor<Organizer>, org.springframework.data.repository.Repository<Organizer,String>
public interface OrganizerRepository extends org.springframework.data.mongodb.repository.MongoRepository<@NonNull Organizer,@NonNull String>
MongoDB Repository for managing Organizer entities.

This interface handles operations specific to individual organizers (people), utilizing the polymorphic nature of the "Organizers" collection.

    • findByUsername

      Optional<Organizer> findByUsername(String username)

    • existsByUsername

      boolean existsByUsername(String username)
      Checks if an Organizer with the given username already exists.
      Parameters:
      username - The username to check.
      Returns:
      true if exists, false otherwise.

      org.springframework.data.domain.Page<@NonNull Organizer> findByType(String type, org.springframework.data.domain.Pageable pageable)
      Find all the Organizer or all the Organization
      Parameters:
      type - ORGANIZER or ORGANIZATION
      pageable - the page for pagination
      Returns:
      the page

    • getOrganizationAnalisys

      @Aggregation(pipeline={"{\n $match: {\n $or: [\n { \"type\": \"ORGANIZER\", \"organizations.id\": ?0 },\n { \"type\": \"ORGANIZATION\", \"_id\": ?0 }\n ]\n }\n}\n","{\n $group: {\n _id: null,\n organizationTotalEvents: {\n $max: {\n $cond: [\n { $eq: [\"$type\", \"ORGANIZATION\"] },\n { $size: { $ifNull: [\"$events\", []] } },\n 0\n ]\n }\n },\n list: {\n $push: {\n $cond: [\n { $eq: [\"$type\", \"ORGANIZER\"] },\n {\n username: \"$username\",\n name: \"$name\",\n lastname: \"$lastname\",\n events: \"$events\"\n },\n \"$$REMOVE\"\n ]\n }\n }\n }\n}\n","\n {\n $project: {\n _id: 0,\n analysis: {\n $map: {\n input: \"$list\",\n as: \"user\",\n in: {\n username: \"$$user.username\",\n fullname: { $concat: [\"$$user.name\", \" \", \"$$user.lastname\"] },\n organizerEventsCount: { $size: { $ifNull: [\"$$user.events\", []] } },\n organizationTotalEvents: \"$organizationTotalEvents\"\n }\n }\n }\n }\n}\n"}) List<HashMap<?,?>> getOrganizationAnalisys(String organizationId)
      Executes a MongoDB aggregation pipeline to calculate event statistics.

      The pipeline performs the following steps:

      1. $match: Selects the organization document itself and all organizer documents linked to it
      2. $group: Identifies the maximum event count for the organization and collects organizer data into a list
      3. $project: Maps the results into a final structure calculating individual event counts
      and concatenating organizer names.

      Parameters:
      organizationId - The ID of the organization to analyze.
      Returns:
      A list of maps containing 'username', 'fullname', 'organizerEventsCount', and 'organizationTotalEvents'.
